semanticCore: Disdaining, refusing in pride, turning away, wipe tears, or shy
quranicRelevance: Disdaining to be a servant of God (Yastankifa - 'lan yastankifa al-maseehu', 'yastankifoon').

keyDerivations.meaning: To disdain, refuse in pride, turn away haughtily, or deem beneath oneself

figurativeOrRareUsages: لَنْ يَسْتَنْكِفَ الْمَسِيحُ أَنْ يَكُونَ عَبْدًا لِلَّهِ وَلَا الْمَلَائِكَةُ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ (4:172): The Messiah would never disdain to be a servant of God, nor would the close angels (countering Christian/pagan deifications).
summaryEn: The root represents haughty disdain (Istinkaf), refuting claims of Jesus' divinity by stating that neither he nor the angels disdain serving God.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الترفع والأنفة والكبر والاستنكاف، مؤكداً تواضع المسيح والملائكة ونفي استنكافهم عن العبودية الحقة لله (لن يستنكف المسيح).
rawText: اِسْتَنْكَفَ مِنْهُ 10 (KL, * MA) He disdained, or scorned, it; was ashamed of it. (KL, MA.) See 1. تَنَكَّفَ بِمَوْضِعِ كَذَا 5 [He waited, &c.,] i. q. تَلَيَّثَ. (TA in art. صقر, from the “ Nawádir. ”) نَكَفَةٌ : see غُنْدُبَةٌ, and لُغْدٌ. نَكِفَ مِنَالشَّىْءِ 1 , aor. ﹷ; inf. n. نَكَفٌ; and نَكَفَ, aor. ﹹ; and 🡻استنكف; He abstained from, or refused to do, the thing, from disdain and pride. (Msb.)
